metadata.description: Merged UK DESNZ Heat Network Zoning Pilot data
metadata.note: Boundaries are approximate — digitised from PDF. Not for legal or engineering use.

properties.description: Large strategic zone covering Barking town centre, extending south through residential areas to the Thames riverside. The largest zone by area in the borough.
properties.notes: Includes Barking town centre commercial core, significant social housing estates, and southern riverside regeneration area along the Thames

properties.description: Zone in the northern part of the borough centred on Chadwell Heath, encompassing residential and some commercial/retail areas.
properties.notes: Discrete zone in the north of the borough near the boundary with Havering

properties.description: Zone covering parts of Becontree and central Dagenham, including significant social housing areas from the interwar Becontree estate.
properties.notes: Covers part of the historic Becontree housing estate - one of the largest council estates ever built

properties.description: Riverside zone along the north bank of the Thames, associated with the major Barking Riverside regeneration project delivering thousands of new homes.
properties.notes: Includes the Barking Riverside development zone - major regeneration area with up to 10,800 new homes planned. River Thames offers potential water source heat pump opportunity.

properties.description: Linear zone along the Ilford Lane corridor on the western edge of the borough, connecting into the Barking town centre zone.
properties.notes: Mixed residential and commercial strip along the main arterial route

properties.zone_name: Potential Energy Centre - Barking Town Centre
properties.description: Potential energy centre location identified in the DESNZ pilot, located within the Barking town centre zone.
properties.notes: Square symbol shown on original map

properties.description: Dominant strategic zone covering Birmingham city centre and the entire inner ring road area, extending through Handsworth, Lozells, Aston, Nechells, Bordesley, Highgate, Balsall Heath, Edgbaston and Harborne. By far the largest zone in the pilot by area, forming a near-continuous mass across the city core.
properties.notes: Encompasses the Jewellery Quarter, commercial core, university quarter (UoB, BCU), major hospitals (QE, Heartlands), HS2 Curzon Street and multiple regeneration areas. Multiple energy centres identified.

properties.description: Large zone in the north-east of the city extending from Erdington through to Castle Vale and the surrounding residential areas. Elongated corridor zone following major road and residential networks.
properties.notes: Includes Castle Vale estate (significant social housing), Erdington town centre and surrounding suburbs. Potential ASHP heat sources.

properties.description: Zone in the north of the city covering Perry Barr and parts of Kingstanding, centred on the Perry Barr regeneration area around the Alexander Stadium.
properties.notes: Includes Perry Barr regeneration (post-Commonwealth Games 2022 legacy development). Potential for new development-led heat network.

properties.description: Smaller discrete zone north of the city centre in the Witton and Aston area, including Aston Villa FC grounds and surrounding residential development.
properties.notes: Mixed residential and sports/leisure zone.

properties.description: Zone on the north-eastern edge of the city authority boundary in the Sutton Coldfield / Walmley area. Relatively low-density residential with some commercial anchors.
properties.notes: Discrete zone near the northern boundary. Mixed residential and retail.

properties.description: Zone in the south-east of the city covering the Tyseley industrial corridor and South Yardley residential areas. Includes Tyseley Waste to Energy Plant which is a significant potential heat source.
properties.notes: Tyseley Energy Recovery Facility (EfW) is a major potential anchor heat source for this zone.

properties.description: Zone on the eastern edge of the city near Birmingham Airport and NEC, covering Sheldon and the Marston Green fringe.
properties.notes: Proximity to Birmingham Airport and NEC may offer anchor industrial/commercial heat loads.

properties.description: Zone south of the city centre covering Kings Heath and parts of Moseley, centred on the Kings Heath district centre.
properties.notes: Residential and local commercial zone in south Birmingham.

properties.description: Zone in the south-west of the city around Northfield and the Longbridge regeneration area, site of the former MG Rover plant.
properties.notes: Longbridge regeneration site offers significant new development heat network opportunity. Mixed residential and retail.

properties.description: Zone on the western edge of the city in the Harborne and Quinton area, including residential and some university-adjacent development.
properties.notes: Proximity to University of Birmingham western campus areas.

properties.description: Discrete zone extending east from the main city centre zone through Small Heath and Sparkbrook, connecting into the Bordesley Green corridor.
properties.notes: Dense residential zone with significant South Asian community and commercial high street.

properties.zone_name: Energy Centre – Birmingham City Centre Core
properties.description: Potential energy centre in the Birmingham city centre core area.
properties.notes: Square symbol on original DESNZ map – city centre cluster

properties.description: Potential energy centre in the Digbeth/Eastside area, close to Curzon Street HS2 development.
properties.notes: Square symbol on original DESNZ map – Eastside

properties.description: Potential energy centre serving the Lee Bank and Edgbaston area, close to QE Hospital campus.
properties.notes: Square symbol on original DESNZ map – south-west of city centre

properties.description: Potential energy centre in the Ladywood area, west of city centre.
properties.notes: Square symbol on original DESNZ map – Ladywood

properties.description: Potential energy centre in the Northfield / Longbridge area.
properties.notes: Square symbol on original DESNZ map – south of city

properties.name: Bridlington Town Centre
properties.heat_source: Water Source Heat Pump (WSHP) - marine water
properties.key_buildings: Town Hall, Magistrates Court, East Riding Leisure Centre, Quay Academy, Promenade Shopping Centre
properties.energy_centres: EC1 - Moorfield Road Car Park (ERYC); EC2 - East Riding Leisure Centre Car Park (ERYC)
properties.constraints: Rail crossing at western edge near Station Approach Road

properties.name: Bridlington West
properties.heat_source: Deep Geothermal (Sherwood Sandstone Aquifer, 40-50°C at 1000-1500m) and Ground Source Heat Pump (GSHP)
properties.key_buildings: Bridlington and District Hospital, Bridlington School, Bessingby Industrial Estate units, Morrisons
properties.energy_centres: EC3 - Green space adjacent to Bridlington and District Hospital (ERYC, 19,000m²)

properties.name: Sewerby Road
properties.key_buildings: Burlington Schools, Bay Infant School, East Riding College, council-owned social housing

properties.name: Pinfold Lane
properties.key_buildings: New residential developments (all new build)
properties.constraints: Development timing critical for network rollout

properties.name: Headlands School
properties.key_buildings: Headlands School, Martongate Primary School, Bridlington North Library
properties.constraints: Small zone - size and aggregation to be considered

properties.name: Bristol City Centre & Inner Areas
properties.description: The dominant strategic zone covering Bristol city centre, Harbourside, Clifton, Redcliffe, Temple Meads, St Pauls, Stokes Croft, and surrounding inner residential areas. This is the largest and most complex zone with multiple IZO network routes and energy centre locations visible.
properties.anchor_areas: City centre, Harbourside, Temple Meads, Clifton, Redcliffe, Stokes Croft, St Pauls, Broadmead
properties.notes: Complex multi-arm zone. Energy centre markers (purple squares) visible at ~4 locations within zone. IZO network routes and connected buildings/high potential connections shown in green. Approx outer boundary.

properties.name: Filton / North Bristol
properties.description: Large zone in north Bristol / Filton area. Covers the Filton corridor including Filton Abbey Wood, aerospace/tech business parks, and surrounding residential areas. Likely anchored by large employment sites and retail.
properties.anchor_areas: Filton, Filton Abbey Wood, Bristol Parkway area, Patchway, north Bristol business parks
properties.notes: Substantial northern zone. Energy centre marker visible. Approx boundary.

properties.name: Lockleaze / Horfield / Eastville
properties.description: Zone in north-east Bristol covering Lockleaze, Horfield and Eastville residential areas. Likely includes significant social housing estates.
properties.anchor_areas: Lockleaze, Horfield, Eastville, Stapleton Road area
properties.notes: North-east zone distinct from Filton zone. Approx boundary.

properties.name: Hartcliffe / Withywood / South Bristol
properties.description: Large zone in south Bristol covering Hartcliffe, Withywood and surrounding areas. Significant social housing estates and retail anchors. Green connected buildings markers visible suggesting active IZO development.
properties.anchor_areas: Hartcliffe, Withywood, Hengrove, Stockwood, south Bristol retail
properties.notes: Substantial south Bristol zone. Green high-potential connection markers visible within zone boundary. Approx boundary.

properties.name: Kingswood / Staple Hill
properties.description: Zone in east Bristol / South Gloucestershire border area covering Kingswood and Staple Hill. Commercial and residential mix.
properties.notes: Eastern zone, partially in South Gloucestershire. Approx boundary.

properties.description: Strategic zone covering the University of Kent Canterbury campus in the north-west of the city, including adjacent residential areas, Canterbury Innovation Centre and two schools. One IZO identified, supplied by ASHP.
properties.notes: Existing campus heat network (24 buildings) supplied by gas-fired energy centre on Library Road. IZO connects 26 buildings.

properties.description: Large strategic zone covering Canterbury City Centre, including the Westgate area and extending north-east into the Northgate area. Includes Whitefriars Shopping Centre, Canterbury Christ Church University, Canterbury College, and the Kingsmead Depot (Riverside) regeneration development. One IZO identified, supplied by ASHP.
properties.notes: Existing Canterbury Christ Church University campus heat network within zone. Key constraint: Archaeological Significance designation across city centre and River Stour crossing required.

properties.description: Other HNZ to the north-east of the city centre, centred on the Sturry Road area including Sturry Road Community Park. Mix of residential and commercial buildings.
properties.notes: Part of Group 2 other zones north and east of city centre

properties.description: Other HNZ adjacent to and east of the Canterbury Riverside strategic zone, covering City Business Park and surrounding commercial areas.
properties.notes: Commercial/business park zone

properties.description: Other HNZ east of the city centre including St Martin's Hospital and surrounding area along the Littlebourne Road corridor.
properties.notes: Hospital and residential zone east of city centre

properties.description: Other HNZ south of city centre including Kent and Canterbury Hospital and Simon Langton Grammar School. Area dominated by new residential buildings. ASHP primary heat source; GSHP may also be feasible given land availability.
properties.notes: Largest other zone by heat demand. Key anchor loads: Kent and Canterbury Hospital, Simon Langton Grammar School for Girls

properties.description: Other HNZ south-west of the city centre covering Wincheap Industrial Estate area. Dominated by commercial and industrial buildings including Go Outdoors, Dunelm Mill and Stellison Ltd. River Stour nearby offers potential WSHP heat source.
properties.notes: Commercial/industrial zone; River Stour nearby

properties.description: Other HNZ west of city centre near The Canterbury Academy area.
properties.notes: Smaller campus/education-centred zone

properties.description: Other HNZ immediately west of the Canterbury Riverside strategic zone, covering the Harbledown fringe and approach to the city.
properties.notes: Mixed zone on western edge of city centre area

properties.description: Other HNZ south of the city centre and Canterbury Riverside zone, covering areas around the Kent and Canterbury Hospital approach.
properties.notes: Smaller zone south of city centre

properties.zone_name: Energy Centre E1 – University of Kent (Library Road)
properties.description: Existing/potential energy centre at the University of Kent campus on Library Road. Current gas-fired energy centre; ASHP proposed as replacement/supplement.
properties.notes: E1 on DESNZ map. Land: 850m², University of Kent ownership.

properties.zone_name: Energy Centre E2 – University of Kent (Giles Lane Car Park)
properties.description: Potential energy centre at Giles Lane car park on the University of Kent campus. Alternative/supplementary ASHP location.
properties.notes: E2 on DESNZ map. Car park: 9,000m², University of Kent ownership.

properties.zone_name: Energy Centre E3 – Canterbury Riverside (Hawk's Lane Car Park)
properties.description: Preferred potential energy centre for Canterbury Riverside IZO. Located in car park off Hawk's Lane. ASHP proposed.
properties.notes: E3 on DESNZ map. Car park: ~900m². Ownership unknown — requires further investigation.

properties.zone_name: Energy Centre E4 – Canterbury Riverside (Stour Street Land)
properties.description: Alternative potential energy centre for Canterbury Riverside IZO on land off Stour Street.
properties.notes: E4 on DESNZ map. Land: ~950m². Ownership unknown — requires further investigation.

properties.name: Darlington Centre
properties.anchor_loads: Darlington Memorial Hospital, Cornmill Centre, Dolphin Centre
properties.energy_centres: E1 (adj. Town Hall, DBC land), E2 (adj. Central substation, Central Park)
properties.notes: Approx boundary - derived from visual interpretation of pilot report maps

properties.name: DARL_002
properties.anchor_loads: Magnet Ltd, Cummins Engine Plant, Lingfield House, Student Loans Company
properties.notes: Industrial/office zone. Approx boundary.

properties.name: DARL_005
properties.anchor_loads: Education Village, Grange Care Home, Whinfield Primary School
properties.notes: Approx boundary.

properties.name: DARL_006
properties.anchor_loads: Carmel College, Abbey Junior School, Abbey County Infant School
properties.notes: Educational zone. Approx boundary.

properties.name: DARL_007
properties.anchor_loads: West Park Hospital, West Park Academy, West Park Nursery, Aldi
properties.notes: Approx boundary.

properties.name: DARL_011
properties.notes: Small HNZ. Approx boundary.

properties.name: DARL_014
properties.anchor_loads: Greater Faverdale development site (700 homes by 2036)
properties.notes: New development site. Approx boundary.

properties.name: DARL_015
properties.notes: New strategic development site. Approx boundary.

properties.name: DARL_016
properties.notes: New development site. Approx boundary.

properties.name: DARL_017
properties.notes: Approx boundary.

properties.name: DARL_018
properties.anchor_loads: Argos, Cepac Packaging Ltd, Subsea Innovation Ltd
properties.notes: Industrial/office zone. Approx boundary.

properties.name: DARL_020
properties.notes: Small HNZ. Approx boundary.

properties.description: Large strategic zone covering Leicester city centre and extending east and south-east through dense residential and commercial areas. The dominant zone by area and heat demand in the city.
properties.notes: Encompasses the city core, university quarter, and substantial residential areas to the south and east. Multiple energy centre locations identified.

properties.description: Discrete zone on the western edge of the city, covering the Braunstone and New Parks residential areas. Separated from the main city centre zone by open land and road infrastructure.
properties.notes: Primarily social and residential housing estate area. Relatively self-contained zone with potential for an independent heat network.

properties.zone_name: Potential Energy Centre – City Centre North
properties.description: Potential energy centre location in the northern part of the city centre zone.
properties.notes: Square symbol shown on original DESNZ map — northern city zone

properties.zone_name: Potential Energy Centre – City Centre East
properties.description: Potential energy centre location in the eastern part of the city centre zone.
properties.notes: Square symbol shown on original DESNZ map — east of city centre

properties.zone_name: Potential Energy Centre – University Quarter South
properties.description: Potential energy centre location near the university quarter in the southern part of the main zone.
properties.notes: Square symbol shown on original DESNZ map — southern/university area

properties.description: Potential energy centre location within the western Braunstone/New Parks zone.
properties.notes: Square symbol shown on original DESNZ map — western zone

properties.description: Potential energy centre location on the far eastern edge of the main city zone.
properties.notes: Square symbol shown on original DESNZ map — eastern boundary

properties.name: Liverpool City Centre & Waterfront
properties.description: Large strategic zone covering Liverpool city centre, waterfront/docklands, and extending south through Toxteth and Dingle. Includes the historic commercial district, Liverpool ONE, and the riverside regeneration corridor.
properties.anchor_areas: City centre, Waterfront/docks, Liverpool ONE, Lime Street, Toxteth
properties.notes: Approx boundary - largest zone covering central Liverpool from the waterfront east to the inner ring. Energy centre markers visible near city centre and northern docks area.

properties.name: North Liverpool / Everton / Vauxhall
properties.description: Zone covering north Liverpool including Everton, Vauxhall and the northern docklands regeneration areas. Includes significant social housing stock and new development sites.
properties.notes: Approx boundary. Distinct northern zone extending up towards Bootle boundary.

properties.name: Toxteth / South Liverpool
properties.description: Zone covering Toxteth and southern residential areas of Liverpool, extending towards Aigburth and the river frontage.
properties.anchor_areas: Toxteth, Dingle, Aigburth, south Liverpool riverfront
properties.notes: Approx boundary. Extends from southern edge of city centre zone southward.

properties.name: Anfield / Tuebrook
properties.description: Zone in north-east Liverpool covering Anfield and Tuebrook residential areas. Includes Anfield stadium area.
properties.notes: Smaller zone. Approx boundary.

properties.name: Knotty Ash / Broadgreen
properties.description: Zone in east Liverpool around Broadgreen and Knotty Ash. Likely anchored by Broadgreen Hospital.
properties.notes: Smaller discrete zone in east Liverpool. Approx boundary.

properties.name: Speke / Garston
properties.description: Large zone in south Liverpool covering Speke and Garston, including industrial/commercial areas and significant residential estates. Close to Liverpool John Lennon Airport.
properties.anchor_areas: Speke, Garston, Speke industrial estate, Hunts Cross
properties.notes: Large southern zone. Approx boundary.

properties.name: Wavertree / Edge Hill
properties.description: Zone in inner east Liverpool covering Wavertree and Edge Hill, close to the Royal Liverpool University Hospital and university campuses.
properties.anchor_areas: Wavertree, Edge Hill, Royal Liverpool Hospital, University of Liverpool area
properties.notes: Approx boundary for inner east zone.

properties.description: Very large strategic zone covering the northern riverside (Bankside, Borough, Bermondsey, London Bridge), through Elephant & Castle and into the central residential areas of Walworth, Camberwell and Peckham. This is one of the largest heat network zones in the DESNZ pilot programme by area.
properties.notes: Encompasses the entire densely built northern and central Southwark, including significant social housing, commercial, retail and mixed-use development. Riverside location provides potential for Thames water source heat pumps.

properties.description: Eastern zone covering Bermondsey, South Bermondsey and Rotherhithe riverside, extending east along the Thames towards Deptford. Includes significant riverside regeneration potential.
properties.notes: Thames riverside location provides significant WSHP potential. Includes Canada Water regeneration area and Surrey Quays.

properties.description: Southern zone covering parts of Camberwell Green, East Dulwich and surrounding residential areas. Discrete zone separated from the main central area.
properties.notes: Primarily residential zone in the south of the borough.

properties.description: Discrete zone in the south of the borough covering Nunhead and parts of Peckham Rye, separated from the main central zone.
properties.notes: Residential zone in south Southwark.

properties.zone_name: Potential Energy Centre – Bermondsey / Old Kent Road
properties.description: Potential energy centre location in the Bermondsey / Old Kent Road area as identified on DESNZ pilot map.
properties.notes: Square symbol on original DESNZ map.

properties.name: Strood Town Centre
properties.anchor_loads: Strood Sports Centre, Strood Waterfront development, Nuffield Health Club
properties.energy_centres: E1 (Strood Waterfront dev land, WSHP/GSHP), E2 (Strood Waterfront dev land, ASHP), E3 (Council land adj leisure centre, ASHP), E4 (Diggerland site, ASHP/GSHP)
properties.waste_heat_sources: Whitewall Creek WWTW, UKPN substation, sewers, supermarket chiller systems
properties.constraints: C1: Rail line - 3 crossing points required
properties.notes: Zone extends along north bank of River Medway from Strood Retail Park (SW) to Medway City Estate (NE). Approx boundary derived from visual interpretation of pilot report maps.

properties.name: STRD_002
properties.anchor_loads: Strood Academy, Bligh Primary School, Bligh Children's Centre
properties.notes: Small discrete educational zone. Approx boundary.

properties.name: Bridlington Town Centre
properties.heat_source: WSHP - North Sea marine water, 1500kWp at 10°C
properties.key_buildings: Town Hall; Magistrates Court; East Riding Leisure Centre (2560 MWh/yr); Quay Academy; Promenade Shopping Centre
properties.energy_centres: EC1 - Moorfield Road Car Park 8000m2 (ERYC); EC2 - Leisure Centre Car Park 1250m2 (ERYC)
properties.key_constraints: Rail crossing at western edge near Bridlington Station

properties.name: Bridlington West
properties.heat_source: Deep Geothermal 5000kWp at 45°C (Sherwood Sandstone, 1000-1500m); GSHP 1400kWp at 10°C
properties.key_buildings: Bridlington & District Hospital (2025 MWh/yr); Bridlington School (1800 MWh/yr); Bessingby Industrial Estate; Morrisons
properties.energy_centres: EC3 - Green space adjacent to Hospital 19000m2 (ERYC)

properties.name: Sewerby Road
properties.key_buildings: Burlington Schools; Bay Infant School; East Riding College; council-owned social housing

properties.name: Pinfold Lane
properties.key_buildings: New residential developments (northern boundary of Bridlington)
properties.key_constraints: Development timing critical for network rollout

properties.name: Headlands School
properties.key_buildings: Headlands School; Martongate Primary School; Bridlington North Library
properties.key_constraints: Small zone - size and aggregation to be considered

properties.name: Northallerton Town Centre
properties.local_authority: North Yorkshire Council / Hambleton District Council
properties.key_buildings: Friarage Hospital (10300 MWh/yr); Northallerton School & Sixth Form (1300 MWh/yr); County Hall (1300 MWh/yr); North Yorkshire Police (800 MWh/yr); Tesco (700 MWh/yr)
properties.energy_centres: E1 - Friarage Hospital / Bullamoor Memorial Park (NHS owned; GSHP & ASHP)
properties.existing_heat_networks: Friarage Hospital campus heat network (communal, dual fuel and steam boilers)
properties.key_constraints: A167 road crossing at two points (200m and 350m stretches)

properties.name: Standard Way Industrial Estate
properties.local_authority: North Yorkshire Council / Hambleton District Council
properties.key_buildings: Standard Way Industrial Estate - light industrial and commercial warehouse buildings
properties.key_constraints: Limited heat supply options; northwest of town centre

properties.name: Northallerton School & Leisure (NRTH_002)
properties.local_authority: North Yorkshire Council / Hambleton District Council
properties.key_buildings: Northallerton School & Sixth Form College (Brompton Road); Northallerton Leisure Centre; North Yorkshire Council offices
properties.energy_centres: Not identified - open space for heat pumps
properties.key_constraints: Single road crossing; soft-dig trenching possible; potential to combine with Town Centre zone

properties.key_buildings: Cumberland Infirmary, The Lanes Shopping Centre, Citadel Railway Station, Carlisle Market, Carlisle College
properties.energy_centre_location: E1 - Land between A595 and Viaduct Estate Road
properties.constraints: River Caldew crossing, West Coast Main Line railway crossing

properties.key_buildings: Travelex Agency Services, Bendalls Engineering, Stobart Group, Kingstown Retail Park
properties.energy_centre_location: E2 - Green space adjacent to Elliot Drive
properties.location_description: ~1.5km north of City Centre, includes Kingmoor Park and Kingstown Industrial Estate

properties.key_buildings: New residential units (10,325 homes planned), new schools, employment sites, Carleton Clinic
properties.energy_centre_location: E3 - Near River Caldew by Carlisle Southern Link Road
properties.location_description: South of city centre, proposed Garden Village development
properties.constraints: Cumbrian Coast Railway Line, River Caldew, West Coast Main Line, River Petteril

properties.notes: Could potentially be connected to neighbouring Kingstown zone

properties.location_description: Between City Centre and St Cuthbert's HNZs
properties.notes: Potential to connect to either strategic zone in future

properties.key_buildings: Pennine Way Primary School, Gillford Centre Pupil Referral Unit

properties.notes: Can be connected to either City Centre or St Cuthbert's HNZ in future

properties.location_description: South of city centre, north of St Cuthbert's Garden Village
properties.notes: Opportunity to extend St Cuthbert's HNZ to connect in future
Potential to connect to City Centre HNZ in future

properties.key_buildings: Royal Lancaster Infirmary Hospital (relocating 2035-2039), Standfast & Barracks, Lansil Industrial Estate
properties.notes: Hospital relocation planned; current site likely to be redeveloped

properties.key_buildings: Morrisons Supermarket, Queen Victoria Hospital, Midland Hotel, retail parks
properties.energy_centre_locations: E2/E3 - Car parks in town centre

properties.key_buildings: New developments: 3,500+ new homes, 3 new schools
properties.energy_centre_location: E4 - WWTW site (United Utilities), fallback E6 within development
properties.location_description: New development area; preferred location for relocated Royal Lancaster Infirmary (2035-2039)
properties.notes: Low LHD due to new build; 'soft-dig' construction opportunities
Campus already has operational heat network; £21m GHNF funding secured for ASHP upgrade operational by 2027
Commercial/light industrial buildings; heat demand data limited
Campus has existing gas-fired heat network due for renewal
Educational buildings and commercial/industrial estate

properties.key_buildings: Templeman Library, Eliot College, Sports Centre, Cornwallis North East, Marlowe Building
properties.energy_centre_locations: E1 - Land near Library Road, E2 - Giles Lane car park
properties.notes: Campus already has existing gas-fired heat network serving 24 buildings; IZO would expand connection

properties.key_buildings: Kingsmead Depot (Riverside) new development, Whitefriars Shopping Centre, Canterbury College, Canterbury Christ Church University
properties.energy_centre_locations: E3 - Car park off Hawk's Lane, E4 - Land off Stour Street
properties.constraints: Archaeological significance in city centre, River Stour crossing
properties.notes: Riverside regeneration site offers early infrastructure installation opportunity

properties.heat_sources_potential: ASHP, potential WSHP from River Stour
properties.key_buildings: Go Outdoors, Dunelm Mill, Stellison Ltd
Kent and Canterbury Hospital, Simon Langton Grammar School for Girls

properties.notes: Smaller zones centred around single campus/estate buildings; relatively small heat demands

properties.zone_name: Metadata Only - No Zone Data Extracted
properties.notes: Uploaded file contains only a map image PDF with council branding and contact metadata. No extractable text with zone boundaries, heat demand data, CapEx, or other attributes was present in the document.
properties.data_source: Heat Network Zone Map - Southwark (Pilot Project Outputs)

properties.zone_name: Metadata Only - No Zone Data Extracted
properties.notes: Uploaded file contains only a map image PDF with council branding and contact metadata. No extractable text with zone boundaries, heat demand data, CapEx, or other attributes was present in the document.
properties.data_source: Heat Network Zone Map - Southampton (Pilot Project Outputs)

properties.description: Large strategic heat network zone covering central and south Leeds, from Kirkstall in the west to Crossgates in the east, Meanwood/Chapeltown in the north to Beeston/Belle Isle in the south.
properties.notes: Covers Leeds city centre, university campus, hospitals, major residential areas. Multiple energy centres and IZO routes identified.
properties.data_source: DESNZ Heat Network Zoning Pilot - Leeds

properties.description: Potential energy centre location in Leeds city centre area
Potential energy centre location in south Leeds / Hunslet area
Large strategic heat network zone covering central Exeter, from St Thomas and Marsh Barton in the southwest to Pinhoe and Monkerton in the northeast.
properties.notes: Covers Exeter city centre, university, hospitals, residential areas including Heavitree, Whipton, St Leonards, Countess Wear. Multiple sub-zones visible.
properties.data_source: DESNZ Heat Network Zoning Pilot - Exeter

properties.description: Potential energy centre location near Monkerton, east Exeter
Large strategic heat network zone covering central and western Hull, from Cottingham/Newland in the north through the city centre and docks area to the south.
properties.notes: Covers Hull city centre, marina, docks, university, residential areas. Extensive coverage of western Hull.
properties.data_source: DESNZ Heat Network Zoning Pilot - Hull

properties.description: Potential energy centre location near Spring Bank / city centre area

Large heat network zone covering central Cheltenham, from Swindon Village in the north through the town centre, Pittville, St Peter's, Montpellier, and south to Leckhampton and Charlton Kings. Multiple connected and satellite zones.
properties.notes: Extensive coverage including town centre commercial core, Pittville, The Park, Montpellier, Bayshill, St Mark's, Warden Hill, and Leckhampton. Several satellite zones in Charlton Kings and eastern suburbs.
properties.data_source: DESNZ Heat Network Zoning Pilot - Cheltenham

properties.description: Satellite heat network zone in Charlton Kings, east of Cheltenham town centre.
Large strategic heat network zone covering central Peterborough, from Werrington and Walton in the north through the city centre, Fletton, and Woodston to Hampton and Orton in the south.
properties.notes: Covers city centre, cathedral quarter, Fletton Quays regeneration, New England, Millfield, Eastgate, Fengate industrial area. Extends south to Orton Waterville, Orton Longueville, and Hampton.
properties.data_source: DESNZ Heat Network Zoning Pilot - Peterborough

properties.description: Potential energy centre location in Fengate area, east Peterborough.

Very large strategic heat network zone covering most of Sheffield, from Stannington and Hillsborough in the northwest through the city centre to Handsworth and Woodhouse in the east, and from Ecclesfield in the north to Norton and Graves Park in the south.
properties.notes: Extensive coverage of Sheffield including city centre, universities, hospitals, major residential areas across Walkley, Crookes, Broomhill, Nether Edge, Heeley, Manor, Gleadless, Intake, Handsworth. Veolia already operates district energy network in city centre.
properties.data_source: DESNZ Heat Network Zoning Pilot - Sheffield

properties.description: Potential energy centre location in Sheffield city centre, near existing Veolia district energy network.

Very large strategic heat network zone covering central and western Nottingham. From Basford and Bulwell in the north through the city centre, The Park, Radford, and Lenton to Beeston and Wilford in the south. East to Sneinton and St Ann's.
properties.notes: Covers Nottingham city centre, universities (UoN, NTU), Queen's Medical Centre, Castle, The Park, Lace Market, Sneinton, St Ann's, Radford, Hyson Green, Sherwood, Carrington, New Basford, Old Basford. Existing district heating in city centre area.
properties.data_source: DESNZ Heat Network Zoning Pilot - Nottingham

properties.description: Potential energy centre location south of Nottingham city centre, near Meadows/Trent Bridge area.

Large strategic heat network zone covering central Plymouth from Devonport and Keyham in the west through Stonehouse, the city centre, and Mutley to Lipson and Laira in the east. North to Crownhill and Derriford, south to the Hoe and Cattedown.
properties.notes: Covers Plymouth city centre, The Hoe, Barbican, Devonport Dockyard area, Stonehouse, Stoke, Mutley, Mannamead, Lipson, Laira, Cattedown. Multiple satellite zones including North Prospect, Crownhill/Derriford, and eastern suburbs (Plympton, Elburton).
properties.data_source: DESNZ Heat Network Zoning Pilot - Plymouth

properties.description: Northern satellite zone covering Derriford Hospital, Crownhill, and surrounding areas.
Potential energy centre location south of Plymouth city centre.

Large strategic heat network zone covering central Coventry from Whoberley and Allesley in the west through the city centre to Walsgrave and Binley in the east. From Holbrooks and Radford in the north to Stivichall and Green Lane in the south.
properties.notes: Covers Coventry city centre, ring road area, Cathedral Quarter, university campuses (Coventry and Warwick nearby), hospitals (UHCW), Hillfields, Gosford Green, Stoke, Earlsdon, Chapelfields, Radford, Canley. Multiple satellite zones including Coundon, Tile Hill, and Willenhall.
properties.data_source: DESNZ Heat Network Zoning Pilot - Coventry

properties.description: Western satellite zone covering Tile Hill and Canley areas, including University of Warwick campus.
Potential energy centre location south of Coventry city centre.
